recipes.title: Tabbouleh
recipes.description: A bright Levantine salad of finely chopped parsley, tomatoes, mint and bulgur, dressed with lemon juice and olive oil.
steps: Rinse the bulgur and soak it in cold water for 10 minutes, then drain and squeeze dry.
Wash and thoroughly dry the parsley and mint, then finely chop the leaves with a sharp knife.
Dice the tomatoes very small and finely slice the spring onions.
Combine the herbs, tomatoes, onions and bulgur in a large bowl.
Whisk lemon juice, olive oil, salt and pepper together and pour over the salad.
Toss gently and serve immediately with romaine leaves for scooping.

recipes.title: Fattoush
recipes.description: Crisp Lebanese chopped salad of seasonal vegetables, herbs and toasted pita brightened with sumac and pomegranate molasses.
steps: Tear the pita into pieces, brush with olive oil and toast until golden and crisp.
Chop the lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes and radishes into bite-size pieces.
Mince the garlic and whisk it with lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, olive oil, sumac and salt.
Combine the vegetables and torn mint leaves in a large bowl.
Pour over the dressing and toss to coat just before serving.
Top with the crisp pita chips and an extra dusting of sumac.

recipes.title: Kibbeh
recipes.description: Football-shaped croquettes of bulgur and minced lamb stuffed with spiced ground meat, pine nuts and onions, then deep-fried until crisp.
steps: Soak bulgur in cold water for 20 minutes then squeeze out the excess moisture.
Process the lean lamb, one onion and bulgur in a food processor with salt and half the spices to a smooth paste.
Sauté the second chopped onion in ghee, add the filling lamb, pine nuts and remaining spices, and cook until browned.
Wet your hands and shape the kibbeh shell into ovals with a hollow center.
Stuff each shell with cooled filling and pinch the ends into a football shape.
Deep-fry the kibbeh in 180°C oil for 4-5 minutes until deep golden, then drain on paper towels.

recipes.title: Manakish
recipes.description: Round flatbreads topped with za'atar and olive oil, baked until the edges are blistered and the bread is fragrant.
steps: Mix flour, yeast, salt and sugar, then add water and olive oil to form a soft dough.
Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic, then cover and let rise for 1 hour.
Stir the za'atar with the topping olive oil to a thick paste.
Divide the dough into 8 balls, roll each into a 15 cm disc and place on a tray.
Spread a generous layer of za'atar paste over each disc, leaving a small rim.
Bake at 230°C for 7-9 minutes until the bottoms are pale gold and the topping is fragrant.

recipes.title: Mujaddara
recipes.description: Humble pilaf of lentils and rice crowned with deeply caramelized fried onions, served with a dollop of yogurt.
steps: Rinse the lentils, cover with water and simmer 15 minutes until just tender, then drain.
Slice the onions thinly and fry in olive oil over medium heat for 20 minutes until deep brown and crisp.
Reserve half the onions for garnish and leave the rest with their oil in the pan.
Add rinsed rice, cumin, coriander, salt and pepper to the pan and toast for 2 minutes.
Stir in the lentils and 600 ml of water, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer for 18 minutes on low.
Rest off the heat for 10 minutes, fluff with a fork and top with the reserved crisp onions.

recipes.title: Shish Taouk
recipes.description: Marinated chicken skewers grilled over charcoal until smoky and juicy, traditionally served with garlic toum and pita.
steps: Cut the chicken into 3 cm cubes and place in a large bowl.
Crush the garlic and whisk with yogurt, lemon juice, tomato paste, spices, oil and salt.
Toss the chicken in the marinade, cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
Thread the chicken onto metal skewers, leaving small gaps between pieces.
Grill over hot charcoal or a high griddle for 4 minutes per side until char-marked and cooked through.
Rest for 5 minutes and serve with toum, pita and pickled turnips.

recipes.title: Sayadieh
recipes.description: Levantine fisherman's dish of spiced rice cooked with caramelized onion stock, topped with golden-fried fish and toasted pine nuts.
steps: Slice the onions and fry in olive oil for 25 minutes until very dark, then blend half with stock.
Season the fish with cumin, salt and lemon zest, then pan-fry until just cooked and set aside.
Toast rice in oil with caraway and turmeric, then pour in the dark onion stock.
Cover and simmer for 18 minutes until the rice is tender and amber-colored.
Toast pine nuts in a dry pan until golden.
Mound the rice on a platter, top with fish and pine nuts and serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Knafeh
recipes.description: Hot cheese pastry layered with shredded phyllo and soaked in orange-blossom syrup, a celebrated Levantine dessert.
steps: Soak the akkawi cheese in cold water for 4 hours, changing the water twice to reduce salt.
Make syrup by simmering sugar, water and lemon juice for 10 minutes, then stir in blossom and rose waters and cool.
Shred the kataifi finely and mix with melted butter until fully coated.
Press half the buttered pastry into a 30 cm tray, top with sliced cheese, then cover with remaining pastry.
Bake at 200°C for 30-35 minutes until the bottom is deep golden, flipping halfway if possible.
Pour cool syrup over the hot knafeh, scatter with pistachios and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Baba Ghanoush
recipes.description: Smoky charred eggplant dip blended with tahini, garlic and lemon, drizzled with olive oil and pomegranate seeds.
steps: Char the whole eggplants over an open flame, turning often for 12-15 minutes until the skins blister and collapse.
Place charred eggplants in a colander and let them drain for 20 minutes.
Peel away the burnt skin and discard, keeping the smoky flesh.
Mash the flesh with crushed garlic, tahini, lemon juice and salt to a chunky purée.
Spread on a plate, drizzle with olive oil, and scatter with parsley and pomegranate seeds.
Serve at room temperature with warm pita.

recipes.title: Hummus
recipes.description: Silky purée of chickpeas, tahini, lemon and garlic, finished with olive oil and a sprinkle of paprika.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight with baking soda, then drain and rinse.
Cover with fresh water and simmer for 60-90 minutes until very soft, skimming foam.
Drain the chickpeas, reserving a cup of cooking liquid.
Blend chickpeas with garlic, lemon juice, tahini and salt for 4 minutes until ultra-smooth.
Stream in ice water with the motor running until light and creamy.
Spread on a plate, swirl with olive oil and dust with paprika before serving.

recipes.title: Falafel
recipes.description: Crisp herb-flecked fritters of soaked chickpeas and fava beans deep-fried until shatteringly crisp outside and fluffy within.
steps: Soak chickpeas and fava beans in plenty of cold water for 24 hours, then drain very well.
Pulse the legumes with onion, garlic, herbs, cumin, coriander and salt to a coarse, sandy texture.
Stir in baking soda and refrigerate the mixture for 30 minutes.
Shape into walnut-sized balls or patties, pressing sesame seeds onto the surface.
Fry in 175°C oil for 3-4 minutes until deep mahogany and crisp.
Drain and serve in pita with tahini, tomato and pickles.

recipes.title: Baklava
recipes.description: Layered phyllo pastry packed with chopped pistachios and walnuts, baked golden then drenched in fragrant sugar syrup.
steps: Simmer sugar, water and lemon juice for 12 minutes, then add the floral waters and chill the syrup.
Pulse pistachios and walnuts to a coarse crumb.
Brush a baking tray with melted butter, layer 10 sheets of phyllo, brushing each with butter.
Spread the nut mixture evenly, then layer 10 more buttered sheets on top.
Cut into diamonds, pour the remaining butter over and bake at 170°C for 50 minutes until deep gold.
Pour cold syrup over the hot baklava and let it absorb for at least 4 hours before serving.

recipes.title: Warak Enab
recipes.description: Tender vine leaves rolled around lemony rice and herbs, simmered slowly in a citrus broth until silky.
steps: Rinse vine leaves under cold water and pat dry, trimming the stems.
Mix rinsed rice with chopped tomatoes, herbs, half the oil, lemon juice, allspice and salt.
Place a leaf vein-side up, add a teaspoon of filling, fold the sides and roll tightly.
Line a heavy pot with extra leaves and pack the rolls in tight, snug layers.
Pour over remaining oil, lemon juice and water, weight with a plate, cover and simmer gently for 50 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes off the heat and serve warm or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Sfiha
recipes.description: Open-faced flatbreads from Baalbek topped with spiced minced lamb, tomato and a hit of pomegranate molasses.
steps: Mix flour, yeast, salt, water and oil into a soft dough, knead 8 minutes and let rise 1 hour.
Finely chop the onion and tomato and squeeze out their excess juice.
Combine lamb, onion, tomato, tahini, molasses, allspice and salt and mix well.
Divide the dough into 24 small balls and roll each into a 10 cm round.
Top each round with a generous tablespoon of meat mixture, leaving a thin border.
Bake at 240°C for 8-10 minutes until the dough is set and the meat is sizzling.

recipes.title: Fatteh
recipes.description: Layered dish of toasted pita, warm chickpeas and garlicky yogurt, finished with butter-toasted pine nuts.
steps: Tear pita into pieces, toast at 200°C for 6 minutes until shatteringly crisp.
Warm the chickpeas in their cooking liquid with a pinch of salt and the cumin.
Whisk yogurt with tahini, crushed garlic, lemon juice and salt.
Toast pine nuts in ghee until golden brown, then remove from heat.
Layer pita in a serving bowl, top with drained warm chickpeas, then spoon over the yogurt sauce.
Pour the buttery pine nuts over and serve at once while still warm.

recipes.title: Lahmacun
recipes.description: Paper-thin flatbread spread with seasoned minced lamb, tomatoes and peppers and baked at high heat to a crisp.
steps: Combine flour, yeast, salt and water into a smooth dough; let rest 1 hour.
Pulse pepper, tomato, onion, parsley, lamb, tomato paste, sumac, Aleppo pepper and salt to a wet paste.
Divide dough into 8 balls and roll each as thinly as possible into a 25 cm round.
Spread a thin layer of meat paste over each round, almost to the edge.
Bake on a preheated stone or tray at 260°C for 5-7 minutes until the edges curl and crisp.
Squeeze with lemon, scatter parsley and roll up to eat.

recipes.title: Toum
recipes.description: Fluffy emulsified Lebanese garlic sauce, snowy white and sharp, traditionally served with charcoal-grilled meats.
steps: Peel the garlic and pat the cloves completely dry.
Place garlic and salt in a tall food processor and blitz to a fine paste, scraping the sides.
With the motor running, drizzle a thin stream of oil for 2 minutes until pale and thick.
Add a teaspoon of lemon juice, then continue alternating oil and lemon for 12-15 minutes total.
If the sauce loosens, drop in a teaspoon of ice water to bring it back together.
Transfer to a jar, seal and refrigerate; the flavor mellows after a day.

recipes.title: Kafta
recipes.description: Hand-mixed minced lamb skewers laced with parsley, onion and warm spices, charcoal-grilled until smoky.
steps: Grate the onion and squeeze out as much liquid as possible.
Finely chop the parsley and combine with the lamb, onion and spices.
Knead the mixture for 5 minutes until sticky and elastic.
Wet your hands and shape the meat onto flat metal skewers in long oblongs.
Brush with oil and grill over hot charcoal for 3-4 minutes per side.
Rest briefly and serve with grilled tomato, onion and pita.

recipes.title: Kousa Mahshi
recipes.description: Tender courgettes hollowed and stuffed with rice and minced meat, simmered in a tomato broth until meltingly soft.
steps: Slice the tops off the courgettes and core them with a long thin scoop, leaving a thin shell.
Combine rinsed rice, lamb, 1 tablespoon tomato paste, half the spices and salt.
Stuff each courgette two-thirds full to leave room for the rice to expand.
Lay them snugly in a heavy pot, scattered with chopped tomatoes.
Whisk remaining tomato paste, water, dried mint and salt; pour over the courgettes.
Cover and simmer gently for 45 minutes until the rice is fluffy and the courgettes are tender.

recipes.title: Moutabal
recipes.description: Creamy charred eggplant dip whipped with tahini, yogurt and lemon, smokier and richer than baba ghanoush.
steps: Char the eggplants over an open flame for 12-15 minutes, turning until evenly blackened and collapsing.
Cool slightly, then peel and drain in a sieve for 20 minutes.
Mash the flesh with the side of a knife until creamy but still textured.
Whisk in tahini, yogurt, crushed garlic, lemon and salt to taste.
Spread on a plate, drizzle with olive oil and scatter with chopped mint.
Serve at room temperature with warm flatbread.

recipes.title: Maamoul
recipes.description: Buttery semolina shortbread cookies stamped in wooden molds and filled with date paste or nuts, eaten on Easter and Eid.
steps: Combine semolina and flour with melted butter and rest overnight at room temperature.
Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm milk with the floral waters and knead into the semolina mixture.
Mix date paste with cinnamon and roll into walnut-sized balls; do the same with the ground walnuts mixed with sugar.
Take a piece of dough, flatten, place a filling ball in the center and seal completely.
Press into a maamoul mold to imprint the pattern, then tap out onto a baking sheet.
Bake at 170°C for 18-20 minutes until just set but still pale, then cool and dust with powdered sugar.

recipes.title: Shanklish
recipes.description: Aged spiced cheese balls rolled in za'atar and chili, crumbled over tomato, onion and olive oil for a piquant mezze.
steps: Crumble the shanklish balls coarsely into a shallow bowl.
Finely dice the tomato and onion and chop the herbs.
Add the tomato, onion, parsley and mint to the cheese.
Drizzle with olive oil, lemon juice and Aleppo pepper.
Toss gently so the cheese stays in chunky pieces.
Serve at room temperature with warm pita to scoop.

recipes.title: Riz a Djej
recipes.description: Festive Lebanese spiced rice studded with toasted nuts and shredded chicken, perfumed with cinnamon and allspice.
steps: Simmer the chicken with whole spices and salt for 60 minutes, then strain and reserve the broth.
Shred the chicken meat from the bones and discard the skin.
Brown the minced lamb in ghee with allspice and cinnamon for 8 minutes.
Stir in rinsed rice, toast 2 minutes, then pour over 800 ml hot broth.
Cover and simmer 18 minutes until the rice is tender, then rest 10 minutes.
Toast pine nuts and almonds in a little ghee, mound rice on a platter and top with shredded chicken and nuts.

recipes.title: Labneh
recipes.description: Strained yogurt cheese with the tang of fresh dairy, drizzled with olive oil and dusted with za'atar to scoop with pita.
steps: Stir the salt thoroughly into the yogurt.
Line a sieve with a clean cheesecloth set over a bowl.
Tip in the yogurt, fold the cloth over and weight with a small plate.
Refrigerate for 24 hours, draining off the whey occasionally.
Spread the thick labneh into a swooped layer on a plate.
Pour over olive oil, sprinkle with za'atar and garnish with olives.

recipes.title: Kibbeh Nayyeh
recipes.description: Raw lamb pounded with fine bulgur and spices to a silky pâté, dressed with olive oil and torn mint to scoop with bread.
steps: Soak bulgur in cold water for 15 minutes, then drain and squeeze dry.
Trim every trace of fat and sinew from the very fresh lamb and dice it.
Pulse lamb, onion, spices and salt in a food processor with ice water until silky.
Pulse in the bulgur until it forms a smooth, cohesive paste.
Spread on a chilled plate and score the surface with a fork.
Drizzle generously with olive oil and scatter with mint, serving immediately with cold pita.
